heading: Title
text: # Contract Audit Checklist

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Define a repeatable audit checklist for contract files, approvals, obligations, changes, and retention. The checklist helps verify that contract records are complete, searchable, approved, and ready for internal or external review.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope This checklist applies to active contracts, expired contracts within retention period, high-risk contracts, material amendments, addenda, variations, subcontracting approvals, and contracts selected for periodic file-quality review.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - Audit evidence must be linked to the contract record, not stored only in email. - Audit scope should include approvals, signed copies, obligations, renewals, and changes. - High-risk contracts should receive deeper review than low-risk template contracts. - Audit findings must have owners and target resolution dates. - Retention must be checked against the 7-year minimum UAE FTA and commercial-law requirement.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework | Audit area | Evidence to check | Pass criteria | |---|---|---| | Register completeness | Contract ID, counterparty, owner, value, term, renewal, status, risk level. | Mandatory fields are complete and current. | | Executed copy | Signed PDF or electronic signature envelope. | Final signed version is stored and linked. | | Approval trail | DoA, finance, privacy/security, procurement, legal, exception approvals. | Approvals match value, risk, and deviation profile. | | Change control | Addenda, variations, PO amendments, delegation or subcontracting approvals. | Changes are documented, approved, and linked to the original contract. | | Obligations | Clause references, owner, due date, frequency, status, evidence. | Operational obligations are assigned and monitored. | | Renewal management | End date, notice date, auto-renewal status, owner decision. | Renewal decision is made before notice deadline. | | Retention | Archive path and retention date. | Records are retained for 7 years minimum. |

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Contract manager | Runs audit, samples files, records findings, tracks remediation. | | Business owner | Confirms operational status, obligation evidence, and renewal decisions. | | Legal counsel | Reviews legal exceptions, addenda, disputes, and high-risk deviations. | | Finance / procurement | Confirms value, PO, payment, and DoA evidence. | | Privacy / security | Confirms data-processing approvals and security evidence. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Audit sample list. - Contract register export. - Executed contract file. - Approval trail. - Obligation tracker. - Change/addendum record. - Renewal decision evidence. - Audit findings log.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Is the signed version linked and readable? - Are mandatory register fields complete? - Does approval evidence match risk and value? - Are addenda and variations traceable? - Are obligations assigned to real owners? - Are overdue obligations escalated? - Are renewal notice dates visible? - Is retention documented?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Missing signed copy or wrong version linked. - Approval trail exists in email but not in the contract file. - Contract value differs between register, PO, and executed agreement. - Addendum signed but register not updated. - Obligations not extracted after signature. - Retention date missing.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Audit a monthly sample of high-risk and renewal-near contracts. - Add mandatory remediation owners and due dates. - Use exception reporting for missing signed copies and approvals. - Link addenda and PO changes to the original contract ID. - Track recurring audit failures as process KPIs.

heading: Title
text: # Contract Change Management Procedure

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Define controls for contract variations, addenda, PO amendments, subcontracting, delegation, assignment, term extensions, and other post-signature changes. The procedure ensures material changes receive proper approval and are traceable to the original contract.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope This procedure applies to all signed contracts and pending contracts where scope, value, term, liability, data processing, subcontracting, assignment, payment, service levels, or termination rights are changed. It includes Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026 Etimad/client-entity approval checks where applicable.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - Contract changes must be documented before implementation. - A PO amendment is not a substitute for a written addendum where contractual rights or obligations change. - Any cumulative contract variation exceeding 10% of the original contract value must trigger formal DoA re-approval and a written Addendum. - For contracts falling under Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026, any variation or delegation of work to a subcontractor must be verified against the Etimad platform and approved by the client entity to avoid systemic breach. - Change records must update the register, risk rating, obligations, and audit trail.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework 1. Log the proposed change with contract ID, change type, business reason, original contract value, cumulative variation value, owner, and required date. 2. Assess whether the change affects scope, value, term, liability, data, subcontracting, assignment, service levels, payment, or termination. 3. Calculate cumulative variation percentage against original contract value. 4. If cumulative variation exceeds 10%, obtain formal DoA re-approval and prepare a written Addendum. 5. If subcontracting, delegation, or assignment is involved, check whether prior notice, consent, Etimad verification, or client entity approval is required. 6. Attach approvals, addendum, updated PO, Etimad/client approval evidence where applicable, and legal review notes to the contract file. 7. Execute the addendum or approved change document. 8. Update the contract register, obligation tracker, renewal calendar, risk matrix, and audit checklist.

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Business owner | Initiates change request and confirms business need. | | Contract manager | Logs change, calculates variation percentage, tracks approvals and evidence. | | Legal counsel | Determines addendum need and reviews contractual impact. | | Finance / procurement | Confirms budget, PO impact, and DoA re-approval. | | Client entity approver | Approves covered Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026 variations/delegations through required process. | | Authorized signatory | Signs addendum after approvals are complete. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Change request record. - Original contract value. - Cumulative variation calculation. - DoA approval where threshold applies. - Written addendum where required. - PO amendment if applicable. - Etimad verification and client entity approval where applicable. - Updated register and obligations.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Is the change logged before implementation? - Is cumulative variation calculated correctly? - Does the change exceed 10% of original contract value? - Is DoA re-approval documented? - Is a written Addendum prepared where required? - Does subcontracting/delegation require notice, consent, or Etimad/client approval? - Are register, obligations, and audit trail updated?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Informal scope changes implemented through email. - PO amendment used despite contract-scope change. - Variation percentage calculated only for the latest change, not cumulative changes. - Subcontracting approved operationally without contract review. - Etimad/client approval evidence missing for covered contracts. - Signed addendum not linked to the original contract record.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Add a change request form to the intake workflow. - Automate variation percentage calculation in the tracker. - Require legal review for all subcontracting, delegation, and assignment changes. - Add mandatory DoA and addendum fields for variations above 10%. - Audit change-control records monthly.

heading: Title
text: # Contract Clause Playbook

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Provide standard negotiation positions, fallbacks, and escalation triggers for recurring contract clauses. This playbook supports faster review while preserving consistency and controlled escalation.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope The playbook applies to legal and commercial negotiation of liability, indemnity, payment, termination, auto-renewal, intellectual property, subcontracting, assignment, data protection, and governing law clauses.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - Preferred positions should be used first. - Fallbacks require documented business and legal rationale. - Escalation triggers must be clear enough for non-lawyers to recognize. - Repeated exceptions should feed template updates. - Subcontracting and assignment must be reviewed against notice, consent, and applicable Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026 requirements.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework | Clause | Preferred position | Fallback | Escalate when | |---|---|---|---| | Liability cap | Fees paid/payable in prior 12 months. | Up to 2x fees for defined claims. | Uncapped liability or broad carveouts. | | Indemnity | Third-party IP, confidentiality, data breach caused by indemnifying party. | Mutual indemnity with procedural controls. | First-party losses or open-ended business claims. | | Payment | Net 30 from valid invoice. | Net 45 with finance approval. | Net 60+, broad setoff, or subjective acceptance. | | Termination | Mutual material breach after cure. | Convenience if economics are protected. | No termination right or long cure for critical breach. | | Auto-renewal | Notice-based renewal with tracked opt-out date. | Renewal allowed with at least 60 days notice. | Notice window under 30 days or price increase without notice. | | IP ownership | Each party keeps pre-existing IP; licenses match deal model. | Limited assignment for paid deliverables. | Broad assignment of tools, know-how, or platform IP. | | Subcontracting / assignment | Subcontracting requires 30 days prior written notice. | Prior written notice for critical trades only. | No subcontracting allowed without consent or contract falls under Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026 requiring explicit upstream approvals via Etimad. | | Data protection | DPA matches data

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: map and operating capability. | Alternative clauses reviewed by privacy/security. | Unsupported transfer terms or breach notice shorter than feasible. |

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Legal counsel | Owns preferred positions, fallback language, and escalation decisions. | | Business owner | Confirms commercial tolerance and operational impact. | | Privacy / security | Reviews data protection, subprocessors, breach, and system-access terms. | | Finance / procurement | Reviews payment, PO, credit, setoff, and commercial concessions. | | Contract manager | Logs deviations and updates the playbook review queue. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Redline showing clause change. - Playbook deviation note. - Fallback approval. - Escalation decision record. - Privacy/security approval where applicable. - Finance approval for payment deviations. - Etimad/client approval evidence where applicable.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Is the proposed clause aligned to preferred position? - If not, is the fallback documented? - Does the issue meet an escalation trigger? - Has the correct approver accepted the deviation? - Does the clause create a new obligation? - Should the template or playbook be updated?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Accepting counterparty fallback without documenting why. - Escalating too late after commercial positions are already promised. - Treating subcontracting as an operational issue only. - Missing privacy/security review for DPA deviations. - Not updating templates after repeated clause concessions.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Review clause exceptions quarterly. - Add approved fallback language to templates. - Track negotiation drivers by clause type. - Train business owners on escalation triggers. - Require written approval for high-risk deviations.

heading: Title
text: # Contract Governance Framework

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Define decision rights, accountability, cadence, and escalation paths for contract management. The framework clarifies who owns commercial decisions, legal positions, approvals, data risk, change control, and post-signature performance.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope The framework applies to all contract lifecycle stages: intake, risk triage, review, negotiation, approval, signature, storage, obligation tracking, renewals, variations, audits, and KPI reporting.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - Governance must assign accountability, not merely participation. - Business owners own commercial outcomes and operational delivery. - Legal owns legal positions and deviation control. - Finance/procurement owns budget, payment, PO, and DoA alignment. - Contract management owns process integrity, records, renewals, and evidence. - Covered Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026 variations and delegations require Etimad/client entity approval checks.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework | Governance layer | Decision rights | Cadence | |---|---|---| | Business owner | Commercial need, scope, operational acceptance, budget ownership. | Per request and monthly obligation follow-up. | | Contract manager | Register quality, renewal alerts, workflow routing, records, audit coordination. | Weekly queue review and monthly governance pack. | | Legal | Template control, negotiation positions, deviations, high-risk terms, addenda. | As needed for reviews; quarterly playbook refresh. | | Finance / procurement | Value, budget, payment terms, PO alignment, DoA compliance. | Per approval threshold and monthly exceptions review. | | Privacy / security | Data processing, system access, security controls, breach and audit terms. | Per data-bearing contract and quarterly high-risk review. | | Executive sponsor | Strategic contracts, high-risk exceptions, disputes, material variations. | Exception-based and quarterly governance review. | | Client entity approver | Etimad approvals for covered contracts. | Per covered variation or delegation. |

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Governance chair | Reviews KPIs, exceptions, overdue actions, and systemic issues. | | Contract manager | Prepares governance pack and tracks action closure. | | Business owner | Reports operational issues, renewals, acceptance, and supplier/customer performance. | | Legal counsel | Reports high-risk clauses, disputes, and template improvements. | | Finance / procurement | Reports value changes, PO exceptions, and DoA compliance. | | Privacy / security | Reports data-bearing contract issues and control gaps. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Governance meeting agenda and minutes. - Monthly contract dashboard. - Renewal action list. - Open obligations and overdue list. - High-risk contract exceptions. - Audit findings and remediation log. - Change-control register. - KPI trend report.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Are decision rights clear by role? - Are monthly governance packs produced? - Are renewal and obligation exceptions escalated? - Are high-risk deviations reviewed? - Are action owners and due dates recorded? - Are Dubai Law No. 5 of 2026 Etimad approval checks captured where applicable?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Many reviewers, no accountable owner. - Governance meeting reviews volume but not risk. - Legal exceptions accepted without template feedback. - Finance approval not linked to addendum or PO change. - Etimad/client entity approval evidence not retained. - KPI trends discussed without corrective actions.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Establish a monthly governance pack with standard sections. - Track action closure between meetings. - Add a high-risk exception register. - Review renewal decisions 120 days ahead. - Require quarterly clause playbook updates from negotiation data. - Include audit and change-control findings in governance reporting.

heading: Title
text: # Contract KPI Framework

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Define practical KPIs for measuring contract workflow health, audit readiness, renewal control, obligation management, and negotiation quality. The KPI framework helps leadership identify bottlenecks and recurring risk drivers.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope The framework applies to contract intake, review, approval, signature, register quality, obligation tracking, renewals, audits, exceptions, and change management. KPIs should be reviewed monthly and trended quarterly.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - KPIs must drive action, not just reporting. - Cycle-time metrics should be segmented by risk level and review lane. - Readiness metrics should focus on evidence completeness. - Renewal and obligation metrics should identify preventable operational risk. - Exception trends should feed template and playbook improvements.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework | KPI | Definition | Target / signal | |---|---|---| | Cycle time | Days from intake submission to signature. | Track median and high-risk outliers monthly. | | First-pass completeness | Percentage of intake requests accepted without missing mandatory fields. | Target 90% or higher after process stabilization. | | Renewal notice compliance | Percentage of renewal decisions made before notice deadline. | Target 100% for auto-renewal contracts. | | Obligation overdue rate | Open obligations past due divided by total open obligations. | Escalate when above 5%. | | High-risk exception rate | High-risk contracts with approved deviations divided by total high-risk contracts. | Review trend and recurring clause drivers quarterly. | | Audit readiness | Contracts with complete executed copy, approvals, owner, value, term, and retention data. | Target 95% completeness. | | Change-control compliance | Material variations with DoA approval and written addendum. | Target 100% for variations above 10%. |

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Contract manager | Produces KPI dashboard, validates source data, tracks corrective actions. | | Business owner | Resolves intake gaps, obligation delays, and renewal decisions. | | Legal counsel | Analyzes negotiation exceptions and updates templates/playbook. | | Finance / procurement | Tracks DoA, PO alignment, and payment-term exceptions. | | Governance chair | Reviews KPI trends and assigns improvement actions. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - KPI dashboard. - Intake queue data. - Contract register data. - Obligation tracker data. - Renewal calendar. - Audit checklist results. - High-risk exception log. - Change-management register.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Are KPIs refreshed monthly? - Are metrics segmented by risk level? - Are source data fields complete enough to trust the KPI? - Are overdue obligations and renewal decisions escalated? - Are high-risk exceptions reviewed for template improvements? - Are KPI actions owned and tracked?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Measuring volume but not cycle time or risk. - KPI data polluted by incomplete register fields. - Renewal compliance measured after deadlines pass. - No owner assigned for improvement actions. - High-risk exceptions approved repeatedly without playbook updates.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Create a monthly dashboard with threshold indicators. - Add data-quality checks before KPI publication. - Trend KPIs for at least three months before setting aggressive targets. - Link KPI exceptions to governance actions. - Use recurring negotiation issues to revise templates and fallback language.

heading: Title
text: # Contract Management Procedure

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Define a consistent end-to-end procedure for requesting, reviewing, approving, signing, storing, and monitoring contracts. The procedure is intended to reduce missed approvals, untracked obligations, unmanaged renewals, and informal changes that bypass the delegation of authority.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope This procedure applies to NDAs, MSAs, SOWs, order forms, amendments, DPAs, vendor agreements, partnership agreements, addenda, and material contract variations. It covers contracts from intake through post-signature governance, including records retention and change control.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - Every contract must have a named business owner. - A contract cannot be marked active until the executed copy, value, risk level, term, renewal date, and owner are recorded. - Risk routing must be based on the highest material risk factor, not only contract value. - Non-standard terms must be tracked before signature, not rediscovered during disputes. - Contract records must be retained for 7 years minimum to fulfill UAE Federal Tax Authority (FTA) and commercial law requirements. - Any cumulative contract variation exceeding 10% of the original contract value must trigger formal DoA re-approval and a written Addendum, rather than a simple PO amendment.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework 1. Open a request in the intake queue with counterparty, contract type, business owner, target signature date, estimated value, data profile, and known non-standard terms. 2. Triage the request using the contract risk matrix and assign a review lane: business review, legal review, privacy/security review, finance review, or executive approval. 3. Draft or review using the approved template where possible. If counterparty paper is used, identify deviations from preferred positions. 4. Negotiate against the clause playbook. Track preferred position, fallback, escalation trigger, decision owner, and open issue status. 5. Confirm approvals before signature: legal, finance, procurement, privacy/security, executive sponsor, and DoA approver as applicable. 6. Confirm the final version is clean, complete, and includes all exhibits, schedules, addenda, and correct legal entity details. 7. Execute the contract using an authorized signatory. 8. Store the signed copy, update the contract register, extract obligations, and schedule renewal notice dates. 9. Monitor obligations, renewals, changes, audit findings, and KPI performance monthly.

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Business owner | Defines business need, confirms scope, accepts deliverables, owns operational performance. | | Contract manager | Runs intake, register quality, routing, renewals, audit queue, and records retention. | | Legal counsel | Reviews legal terms, deviations, addenda, high-risk positions, and escalation decisions. | | Finance / procurement | Confirms budget, payment terms, PO alignment, tax, and DoA compliance. | | Privacy / security | Reviews data processing, system access, security controls, subprocessors, and breach obligations. | | Authorized signatory | Signs only after final version and approval trail are complete. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Completed intake record. - Risk rating and review lane. - Final approved redline or clean contract. - Approval trail and DoA evidence. - Executed copy and signature envelope. - Contract register record. - Obligation tracker entries. - Renewal notice schedule. - Retention/archive location.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Is the business owner named and accountable? - Is the counterparty legal entity correct? - Is the contract value recorded and approved? - Is the risk rating supported by the risk matrix? - Are non-standard terms documented? - Are privacy, security, finance, procurement, and legal approvals complete where needed? - Is the signed version linked in the register? - Are obligations and renewal dates tracked?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Contract requests submitted without value, owner, or target date. - Customer paper reviewed without a deviation summary. - Signed contracts stored outside the register. - Renewal dates entered but notice dates omitted. - Obligations left in legal language without operational owners. - PO amendments used for material changes that require addenda and DoA approval.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Require mandatory intake fields before review starts. - Use a standard clause playbook for repeat negotiation issues. - Add monthly checks for incomplete register fields. - Automate renewal alerts at 120, 90, 60, and 30 days. - Review high-risk exceptions quarterly and update templates. - Train business owners on when contract changes require formal approval.

heading: Title
text: # Contract Register And Obligation Tracker

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Define the records and tracking requirements for active contracts, renewal dates, signed copies, obligations, owners, and evidence. The register and obligation tracker provide the operational backbone for contract governance.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope This document applies to every contract that is drafted, reviewed, signed, amended, renewed, expired, or terminated. It includes signed copies, renewal notices, obligations, audit evidence, and change-control references.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - The register is the single source of truth. - Every active contract must have an owner, value, term, risk level, and signed-copy link. - Obligations must be converted from clause language into operational tasks. - Renewal notice dates must be tracked separately from end dates. - Change-control records must link to the original contract ID.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework Minimum register fields: - Contract ID. - Counterparty. - Contract type. - Business owner. - Status. - Risk level. - Total contract value. - Effective date. - End date. - Renewal notice date. - Auto-renewal flag. - Data involved flag. - Signed copy link. - Key notes. Minimum obligation fields: - Obligation ID. - Contract ID. - Clause reference. - Obligation summary. - Owner. - Frequency. - Due date. - Status. - Evidence or notes.

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Contract manager | Maintains register fields, signed-copy links, renewal alerts, and data quality. | | Business owner | Owns operational obligations and confirms renewal decisions. | | Legal counsel | Identifies legal obligations and high-risk terms for tracking. | | Finance / procurement | Confirms value, PO, payment, and renewal economics. | | Privacy / security | Confirms data-bearing obligations and evidence. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Signed contract file. - Register entry. - Obligation tracker entry. - Renewal decision record. - Approval trail. - Change/addendum references. - Evidence of completed obligations.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Does every active contract have mandatory fields complete? - Is the signed copy linked? - Are notice dates calculated and visible? - Are obligations assigned to owners? - Are overdue obligations escalated? - Are addenda and variations linked? - Is retention/archive information complete?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Contract saved but not entered in the register. - Renewal end date captured but notice date missing. - Obligations assigned to departments rather than named owners. - Evidence of completed obligations not retained. - Addenda stored separately from parent contract.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Freeze mandatory register columns and protect formulas where possible. - Use dropdown lists for status, risk, contract type, and obligation status. - Review renewal windows monthly. - Audit signed-copy links and mandatory fields. - Add alerts for overdue obligations and missing owners.

heading: Title
text: # Contract Risk Matrix

heading: Purpose
text: ## Purpose Provide a structured method to rate contract risk and route contracts to the correct review and approval path. The matrix prevents low-value but high-risk contracts from bypassing legal, privacy, security, or executive review.

heading: Scope
text: ## Scope The matrix applies to all new contracts, renewals, amendments, addenda, variations, subcontracting requests, assignments, and material PO changes. It should be used at intake and refreshed whenever contract scope, value, data use, liability, or operational criticality changes.

heading: Key Principles
text: ## Key Principles - Rate the contract at the highest individual risk factor. - A single high-risk factor is enough to route a contract as high risk. - Risk is not only financial; data, liability, criticality, and template position matter. - Risk rating must be visible in the contract register. - Risk rating should be reassessed before signature and after material change requests.

heading: Procedure Or Framework
text: ## Procedure Or Framework | Risk factor | Low | Medium | High | |---|---|---|---| | Contract value | Low value or no monetary commitment. | Moderate value within normal budget authority. | High value, strategic spend, or revenue impact. | | Data and systems | No personal, confidential, or system access data. | Limited personal or confidential data. | Sensitive, regulated, cross-border, or privileged system access. | | Liability exposure | Standard capped liability and balanced remedies. | Negotiated cap or limited carveouts. | Uncapped liability, broad indemnities, penalties, or liquidated damages. | | Operational criticality | Non-critical, easy replacement, low dependency. | Material support or delivery dependency. | Mission-critical vendor, customer commitment, SLA, or business continuity impact. | | Template position | Company template with minimal changes. | Counterparty paper or moderate deviations. | Heavily negotiated terms, unusual jurisdiction, or high-risk commercial model. | Suggested rating logic: 1. Score each factor as 1 for low, 2 for medium, and 3 for high. 2. If any factor is 3, classify as high risk. 3. If no factor is 3 but the total score is 8 or more, classify as medium risk. 4. Otherwise classify as low risk.

heading: Roles And Responsibilities
text: ## Roles And Responsibilities | Role | Responsibilities | |---|---| | Business owner | Provides accurate value, scope, operational dependency, and timing information. | | Contract manager | Applies the matrix and records the risk level in the register. | | Legal counsel | Confirms risk level for non-standard terms, liability, jurisdiction, and high-risk clauses. | | Privacy / security | Rates data, system access, subprocessors, breach, and audit risk. | | Finance / procurement | Rates value, budget, payment, PO, and supplier concentration risk. |

heading: Evidence Required
text: ## Evidence Required - Completed intake form. - Risk factor scores and suggested rating. - Review lane decision. - Legal/privacy/security notes for medium and high-risk contracts. - Approval trail for high-risk exceptions. - Updated register risk field.

heading: Assessment Checklist
text: ## Assessment Checklist - Has every risk factor been scored? - Does the highest risk factor drive the route? - Is the risk rating consistent with data and system access? - Is the liability position standard or escalated? - Does operational criticality reflect business continuity impact? - Has the rating been refreshed after any material variation?

heading: Common Gaps
text: ## Common Gaps - Rating contracts based only on value. - Treating DPAs or data-bearing contracts as low risk because value is zero. - Failing to reassess risk after scope changes. - No evidence explaining why a high-risk term was accepted. - Missing privacy/security review for vendors with system access.

heading: Recommended Improvements
text: ## Recommended Improvements - Embed the matrix in the intake workflow. - Add mandatory explanations for any high-risk score. - Review high-risk contracts quarterly. - Trend recurring risk drivers and update the clause playbook. - Require executive approval for critical risk exceptions.
